obligations.text: Bureau to prescribe such rules and issue such guidance as may be necessary to carry out, enforce, and compile data pursuant to section 1071
obligations.plain_language: The CFPB must create rules and guidance to implement section 1071 requirements for small business lending data collection and enforcement.
obligations.action_required: Prescribe rules and issue guidance necessary to carry out, enforce, and compile data under section 1071

obligations.text: Financial institutions must collect and report data on small business lending
obligations.plain_language: Banks and other financial institutions are required to gather and submit information about their small business lending activities.
obligations.action_required: Collect and report data on small business lending activities
obligations.trigger_condition: Compliance dates established by the Bureau (subject to court stays in some cases)
obligations.exemptions: Those who are plaintiffs or intervenors in pending litigation may have stayed compliance deadlines

deadlines.action: Final rule implementing section 1071 by adding subpart B to Regulation B published
deadlines.consequence: Rule became effective on this date

deadlines.action: Interim final rule extending compliance dates published in Federal Register
deadlines.consequence: Compliance deadlines extended in accordance with court orders from U.S. District Court for the Southern District of Texas

deadlines.action: Interim final rule published in Federal Register

exemptions.who: Plaintiffs and intervenors in pending litigation
exemptions.condition: In cases where courts have stayed the rule's compliance deadlines
exemptions.section_reference: Document references three jurisdictions with ongoing challenges and court stays

exemptions.condition: Courts did not stay compliance dates for those who are not plaintiffs or intervenors in pending cases
exemptions.section_reference: Document indicates selective application of stays

definitions.definition: Not explicitly defined in this excerpt, but the document indicates the Bureau is amending the small business definition as part of this rulemaking
definitions.relevance: Critical for determining which credit transactions and financial institutions are covered by the reporting requirements

definitions.definition: Provision of the Dodd-Frank Wall Street Reform and Consumer Protection Act directing the Bureau to prescribe rules and guidance for small business lending data collection and enforcement
definitions.relevance: The statutory basis for all requirements in Regulation B, Subpart B

definitions.definition: Rules implementing section 1071 of the Dodd-Frank Act, covering small business lending under the Equal Credit Opportunity Act
definitions.relevance: The regulatory framework being amended by this final rule

obligations.text: financial institutions collect and report to the Bureau certain data regarding applications for credit for women-owned, minority-owned, and small businesses
obligations.plain_language: Financial institutions must gather and submit specific data about credit applications from small businesses and women/minority-owned businesses to the CFPB.
obligations.action_required: collect and report data regarding applications for credit
obligations.trigger_condition: when applications for credit are received for small businesses, women-owned businesses, or minority-owned businesses
obligations.section_reference: Section 1071 of the Dodd-Frank Act, amending ECOA

obligations.text: lenders, improve data quality, and facilitate advance the purposes of section 1071
obligations.plain_language: Covered financial institutions must improve data quality to support the purposes of Section 1071 implementation.

deadlines.consequence: Rule takes effect and compliance obligations begin
All institutions must be in full compliance with Section 1071 requirements

deadlines.action: Comment period closed for 2021 proposed rule
deadlines.consequence: No further comments accepted on 2021 proposed rule

deadlines.action: Final rule published in Federal Register (2025 compliance date final rule)
deadlines.consequence: Final rule confirmed with no further substantive changes

definitions.definition: banks, credit unions, community development financial institutions (CDFIs), community development companies, Farm Credit System (FCS) lenders, online lenders, and other financial institutions subject to Section 1071
definitions.relevance: Defines which entities must comply with Section 1071 data collection and reporting requirements

definitions.definition: Amendment to the Equal Credit Opportunity Act (ECOA) enacted as part of the Dodd-Frank Wall Street Reform and Consumer Protection Act in 2010, requiring collection and reporting of data regarding applications for credit for small businesses, women-owned businesses, and minority-owned businesses
definitions.relevance: Core statutory authority for the regulatory requirements being implemented

definitions.definition: CFPB regulation implementing the Equal Credit Opportunity Act (ECOA)
definitions.relevance: The regulation being amended to implement Section 1071 requirements

definitions.definition: Small Business Regulatory Enforcement Fairness Act process for obtaining feedback from small entities and stakeholders
definitions.relevance: Process used to gather input from affected small businesses and lenders on the rulemaking

obligations.text: facilitate enforcement of fair lending laws
obligations.plain_language: Financial institutions must support efforts to enforce fair lending laws.
obligations.action_required: Cooperate with and facilitate fair lending law enforcement activities

obligations.text: enable communities, governmental organizations, entities, and creditors to identify business and community development needs and opportunities of women-owned, minority-owned, and small businesses
obligations.plain_language: Institutions must provide data and information to help identify lending needs and opportunities for women-owned, minority-owned, and small businesses.
obligations.action_required: Collect and report small business lending data to enable identification of community development needs

obligations.text: The Bureau received approximately 410 comments on the 2025 proposed rule with comment period closed on December 15, 2025
obligations.plain_language: The CFPB accepted public comments on proposed amendments to small business lending rules.
obligations.action_required: Submit comments during designated comment periods
obligations.penalty: Comments submitted after deadline not considered

obligations.text: The Bureau believes that it should approach the section 1071 data collection regime as a longer-term project akin to HMDA
obligations.plain_language: The CFPB intends to implement small business lending data collection as a phased, long-term initiative similar to HMDA.
obligations.who_must_comply: all financial institutions subject to section 1071
obligations.action_required: Prepare for phased implementation of small business lending data collection requirements
obligations.penalty: Non-compliance with future data collection requirements

obligations.text: The Bureau believes that it is a proper use of its authority under 15 U.S.C. 1691c–2 to make changes to several portions of the 2023 final rule to commence data collection with a narrower scope
obligations.plain_language: The CFPB has authority to modify data collection requirements to start with a narrower scope to ensure data quality.
obligations.action_required: Comply with modified data collection requirements as implemented by CFPB
obligations.trigger_condition: When CFPB issues final rule amendments

deadlines.action: Submit comments on 2025 proposed rule amendments to small business lending data collection
deadlines.consequence: Comments submitted after this date will not be considered in the rulemaking process

deadlines.action: CFPB published proposed rule to amend 2023 final rule in Federal Register

deadlines.consequence: Potential impact on compliance deadlines pending court order

deadlines.action: 5th Circuit issued unpublished order tolling compliance deadlines in Texas Bankers Ass'n v. CFPB
deadlines.who: plaintiffs and intervenors in case No. 24–40705
deadlines.consequence: Compliance deadlines tolled until further court order

deadlines.action: E.D. Ky. issued order in Monticello Banking Co. v. CFPB case
deadlines.consequence: Potential tolling of compliance deadlines pending further court order

definitions.definition: Public Law 111–203, tit. X, sec. 1071, 124 Stat. 1376, 2056 (2010), codified at ECOA sec. 704B, 15 U.S.C. 1691c–2
definitions.relevance: Establishes the statutory authority and framework for small business lending data collection requirements under the Equal Credit Opportunity Act

definitions.definition: CFPB regulations implementing the Equal Credit Opportunity Act (ECOA)
definitions.relevance: Governs fair lending practices and data collection requirements for financial institutions

definitions.definition: Specific categories of business entities for which lending data must be collected and analyzed
definitions.relevance: Identifies the focus groups for which community development needs and lending opportunities must be identified

definitions.definition: Home Mortgage Disclosure Act - referenced as a model for phased, long-term data collection implementation
definitions.relevance: Provides a precedent for how the CFPB intends to implement section 1071 data collection as a phased, long-term project

definitions.definition: The CFPB's final rule implementing section 1071 small business lending data collection requirements
definitions.relevance: Establishes baseline requirements that are being modified by the 2025 proposed rule

definitions.definition: Interim rule published by CFPB addressing complexity and scope of small business lending data collection
definitions.relevance: Represents CFPB's interim approach to implementing section 1071 requirements

definitions.definition: Proposed amendments to the 2023 final rule published November 13, 2025, with comment period closing December 15, 2025
definitions.relevance: Represents CFPB's proposed modifications to narrow the scope of data collection to ensure quality

obligations.text: the Bureau now believes that an incremental approach will better serve the statutory purposes of section 1071 in the long term. Such an approach will start with core lending products, core providers, and core data points.
obligations.plain_language: The CFPB will implement section 1071 data collection gradually, beginning with essential lending products, major lenders, and key data points.
obligations.action_required: Comply with an incremental implementation approach starting with core lending products, core lenders, and core data points
obligations.trigger_condition: Implementation of section 1071 small business lending data collection

obligations.text: This approach complies with section 1071 and furthers its statutory purposes while reducing the rule's initial impact on small businesses and lenders.
obligations.plain_language: Financial institutions must implement the incremental approach to comply with section 1071 while minimizing disruption to small business lending markets.
obligations.action_required: Implement data collection in compliance with section 1071 using the incremental approach with limited initial scope
obligations.trigger_condition: Upon effective date of the final rule

obligations.text: The Bureau has consulted with the appropriate prudential regulators and other Federal agencies regarding consistency with any prudential, market, or systemic objectives administered by these agencies
obligations.plain_language: The CFPB coordinated with prudential regulators and other agencies to ensure consistency with their objectives.
obligations.action_required: Coordinate and consult on consistency with prudential, market, and systemic objectives

prohibitions.text: That order, among other things, directs Federal agencies to remove references and questions discussing gender identity. The order also identifies a binary of male/female sex, directing agencies to use those terms when seeking information about an individual's sex.
prohibitions.plain_language: Federal agencies must remove gender identity questions and use only binary male/female sex categories in data collection.
prohibitions.prohibited_action: Including references to or questions about gender identity in federal data collection; using non-binary sex categories
prohibitions.section_reference: Executive Order 14168, Defending Women From Gender Ideology Extremism and Restoring Biological Truth to the Federal Government

deadlines.action: Comply with Executive Order 14168 regarding removal of gender identity references and use of binary sex categories

definitions.definition: Essential small business lending products to be included in the initial phase of data collection under section 1071
definitions.relevance: Defines the scope of products subject to initial reporting requirements

definitions.definition: Primary financial institutions including banks, credit unions, CDFIs, Farm Credit System lenders, and online lenders to be included in initial data collection
definitions.relevance: Identifies which financial institutions must comply with initial reporting requirements

definitions.definition: Essential data elements to be collected in the initial phase of section 1071 implementation
definitions.relevance: Specifies which data fields must be reported in the initial collection phase

definitions.definition: A phased implementation strategy starting with modest requirements focused on core products, lenders, and data points, with potential expansion over time
definitions.relevance: Defines the regulatory implementation methodology for section 1071 compliance

definitions.definition: Statutory provision requiring small business lending data collection under the Equal Credit Opportunity Act (Regulation B)
definitions.relevance: The primary statutory authority governing small business lending disclosure requirements

obligations.text: financial institutions to collect and report to the Bureau certain data on applications for small business lending
obligations.plain_language: Financial institutions must gather and submit specific information about small business loan applications to the CFPB.
obligations.action_required: collect and report data on small business lending applications
obligations.exemptions: smaller lenders may face different requirements based on qualitative differences

obligations.text: The Bureau is issuing this final rule pursuant to its authority under section 1071
obligations.plain_language: The CFPB has the legal authority to create regulations for small business lending data collection under section 1071.
obligations.who_must_comply: all financial institutions subject to section 1071
obligations.action_required: comply with regulations issued under section 1071 authority
obligations.trigger_condition: upon effective date of final rule

obligations.text: Congress amended ECOA by adding section 1071, which directs the Bureau to adopt regulations governing the collection and reporting of small business lending data
obligations.plain_language: The CFPB must create rules that require collection and reporting of small business lending information.
obligations.action_required: adopt and comply with regulations for small business lending data collection and reporting
obligations.trigger_condition: as directed by section 1071 of Dodd-Frank Act

deadlines.action: compliance with final rule on small business lending data collection

exemptions.condition: based on qualitative differences among certain types of lenders and likelihood that smaller lenders would face difficulties in compliance
exemptions.section_reference: section 1071, as discussed in regulatory analysis

definitions.definition: applications for credit by small business entities, subject to collection and reporting requirements under section 1071
definitions.relevance: defines the scope of data that financial institutions must collect and report to the CFPB

definitions.definition: entities required to collect and report small business lending data under section 1071
definitions.relevance: identifies who must comply with data collection and reporting obligations

definitions.definition: provision of the Dodd-Frank Act directing the Bureau to adopt regulations governing collection and reporting of small business lending data
definitions.relevance: primary legal authority for CFPB's rulemaking on small business lending data collection
